macro_knowledge.text: RBI raised the repo rate to 6.5% in June 2026, marking the third consecutive hike to combat persistent inflation above the 6% target. This tightening cycle is expected to increase borrowing costs across sectors.
India's GDP growth is projected at 6.8% for FY 2026-27, down from 7.2% in the previous year. Slower growth is attributed to global headwinds and domestic rate hikes impacting consumption.
Retail inflation (CPI) stood at 6.2% in June 2026, driven by food prices and fuel costs. Core inflation remained elevated at 5.1%, keeping pressure on RBI to maintain hawkish stance.
NSE energy sector outperformed the broader Nifty 50 by 8% in Q2 2026, benefiting from rising crude oil prices and strong refining margins. Reliance Industries led the rally.
IT services sector faced margin pressure in H1 2026 due to wage inflation and cautious client spending in BFSI verticals. TCS and Infosys reported flat revenue growth QoQ.
Private banks maintained asset quality with gross NPA ratios below 3% as of June 2026. However, rising rates are compressing NIMs, with HDFC Bank flagging margin pressure in recent earnings call.
NBFCs face twin headwinds: rising funding costs from bank rate hikes and asset quality stress in unsecured lending portfolios. Cholamandalam and Bajaj Finance reported moderation in AUM growth.

Government's Production Linked Incentive (PLI) scheme for electronics manufacturing is attracting $15B in investments. This is expected to boost manufacturing GDP contribution from 17% to 20% by 2027.

Foreign portfolio investors (FPI) withdrew $4.2B from Indian equities in Q2 2026 due to Fed rate uncertainty and strong dollar. Domestic institutional investors (DII) absorbed the selling, supporting Nifty at 23,000 levels.
INR depreciated 2.3% against USD in H1 2026, trading at 83.5 levels. RBI intervened to prevent sharp volatility, drawing down forex reserves by $8B to $615B.

Brent crude averaged $88/barrel in Q2 2026, up 12% YoY. India's oil import bill increased, widening the current account deficit to 1.8% of GDP. Refining margins remain robust for integrated players like Reliance.
5G subscriber base in India crossed 50 million in June 2026, with Jio capturing 45% market share. ARPU improvements are evident, but capex intensity remains high for telcos.
Rural demand showed signs of recovery in Q2 2026 after two weak quarters. Monsoon forecast is favorable, which could support tractor sales, FMCG volumes, and two-wheeler demand in H2.
Urban housing sales volumes grew 18% YoY in Jan-Jun 2026, driven by metro cities. However, rising home loan rates (now at 9.2% average) are starting to dampen affordability.
Passenger vehicle sales remained flat in H1 2026 due to high waiting periods and semiconductor supply constraints. EV penetration reached 7% of total sales, up from 4% a year ago.
Indian pharma exports to the US grew 9% YoY in Q1 2026, supported by biosimilar launches. However, pricing pressure in generic formulations remains a concern for revenue growth.
Steel prices corrected 7% from Q1 peaks due to weak demand from real estate and infrastructure segments. Tata Steel and JSW flagged EBITDA margin compression.
India's semiconductor policy aims to establish 3 fab units by 2028 with $10B government subsidy. This could create domestic value chains for electronics and reduce import dependence.
Government's capital expenditure on infrastructure is budgeted at ₹10 lakh crore for FY 2026-27, a 15% increase YoY. Roads, railways, and metro projects are key beneficiaries.
Nifty 50 forward PE ratio stands at 21x as of July 2026, above the 5-year average of 19x. Mid-cap and small-cap valuations are stretched at 28x and 32x respectively, raising correction risks.

Credit growth in the banking system moderated to 14% YoY in June 2026 from 16% in March. Corporate loan growth remains tepid while retail loans (especially housing and vehicle finance) drive credit expansion.
Life insurance new business premium grew 12% YoY in H1 2026, with ULIP products gaining traction. Regulatory push for pension penetration is creating long-term growth opportunities.
Kharif sowing area was 8% higher than normal as of early July 2026, supported by timely monsoon arrival. This bodes well for rural income and allied sectors like agrochemicals and tractors.
India's merchandise exports grew 6% YoY to $220B in H1 2026, with engineering goods and pharma leading. Import growth slowed to 4% due to softer gold demand and import curbs on electronics.
Central government's fiscal deficit for FY 2026-27 is targeted at 5.1% of GDP, down from 5.6% in FY 2025-26. GST collections remain robust, averaging ₹1.65 lakh crore per month.
Urban unemployment rate declined to 6.8% in Q1 2026 from 7.2% in Q4 2025. However, labor force participation rate remains subdued at 47%, indicating structural challenges.
Indian startup funding fell 35% YoY in H1 2026 to $6.5B, impacted by global VC caution. Late-stage rounds dried up, forcing several unicorns to defer IPO plans.

India committed to achieve 50% of electricity generation from renewable sources by 2030. Solar capacity additions are running at 15 GW annually, attracting investments from Adani Green, Tata Power, and NTPC.
India-US trade talks progressed in Q2 2026, focusing on tariff reductions in agriculture and services. A bilateral agreement could boost pharma and IT services exports.
India's per capita income crossed $2,800 in FY 2025-26, with middle-class consumption driving demand for discretionary goods, travel, and financial services.
